bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started (t=0.0). The opponent proposed an offer consisting entirely of `valueE`, which provides the absolute minimum utility for me across all issues. To establish a strong anchor and set clear expectations, I will start by proposing my ideal outcome: `valueA` for all issues, targeting my maximum possible utility of 1.00.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.02), but the opponent has made a marginal concession on issueA. I will reciprocate by making a small concession on my lowest-priority issues (issueD and issueE) by moving from valueA to valueB. This maintains my overall utility at a very high 0.96 while signaling a willingness to negotiate.

I will maintain my positions on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to protect my overall utility. To signal a desire for progress, I will make a small concession on my lowest-weight issues, issueD and issueE.

The opponent is repeating their low-utility offer repeatedly while I have made several minor concessions to try and engage them. Time stands at 0.08, so there's no urgency. I will continue to signal small concessions on low-weight issues to keep the negotiation moving, dropping issueD one step while holding firm on my high-priority issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The negotiation is still early at t=0.10. The opponent has held firm and even regressed to their opening offer of all 'valueE'. Since they remain stubborn, I will continue making minor concessions on my least important issues (issueD and issueE) while keeping my highest priority issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) at my preferred 'valueA'. This maintains a high utility for me while demonstrating a willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is holding very firm on mostly 'valueE' across all issues, with only a tiny concession to 'valueD' on issueA. I will make a minor concession on issueC to 'valueB' to signal willingness to negotiate, while keeping my least important issues (D and E) at 'valueD' to see if a compromise can be found without sacrificing too much utility.

The current time fraction is very early (0.16), leaving adequate time for further negotiation. The opponent appears highly inflexible, mostly repeating their baseline offer of 'valueE' across the board, with minor variations on issueA. The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.18). The opponent has been holding consistently firm on their preferred values, alternating only slightly on issueA. I have already fully conceded on my lowest weight issues (issueD and issueE). To demonstrate flexibility without sacrificing my most crucial components, I will now concede one step on my moderately important issue (issueC, weight 0.2) from valueB down to valueC, while holding firm on my highest-weight issues A and B.

The opponent is firmly anchoring on their most preferred values (`valueD` and `valueE` for `issueA`, and `valueE` for everything else) despite my gradual concessions on the less important issues (D and E). The opponent is remaining highly inflexible, consistently offering bids with the lowest possible utility across all issues (mostly `valueE`). I will maintain a high overall utility while attempting a modest concession on `issueB` (an important issue to see if it prompts reciprocity) and keeping low-weight issues `issueD` and `issueE` significantly relaxed.
